title: Hot work near hydrocarbon or gas hazard
excerpt: Hot-work controls should account for nearby flammable gas readings, isolation status, and active permits before work proceeds.

title: Confined-space entry atmospheric checks
excerpt: Confined-space work requires atmospheric monitoring and escalation when toxic or flammable readings trend toward alarm thresholds.

title: Simultaneous operations review
excerpt: Concurrent permits in the same or adjacent zones should be reviewed for spatial and temporal conflict before execution.

title: Shift handover risk continuity
excerpt: Open hazards and active permits should be explicitly carried across shift handover to avoid loss of situational awareness.

title: Incident evidence preservation
excerpt: Safety events should preserve source readings, permit identifiers, timestamps, and operator actions for later review.

title: Gas drift monitoring
excerpt: Gas trends across adjacent zones should be reviewed for drift, not only threshold breach at a single sensor.

title: Energy isolation confirmation
excerpt: Isolation status should be confirmed and linked to active permits before hazardous work continues.

title: Startup abnormal condition review
excerpt: Abnormal process startup conditions should be escalated when instrumentation trends diverge from expected operating envelopes.

title: Tank farm transfer vigilance
excerpt: Tank farm transfers require monitoring for leak, overflow, vapor cloud, and ignition-source exposure.

title: Zone evacuation readiness
excerpt: Escalation procedures should define when workers are moved out of affected and adjacent zones.

title: Emergency communications
excerpt: Safety alerts should be delivered through multiple channels with clear zone, hazard, and action wording.

title: Sensor health and trust
excerpt: Stale, stuck, missing, or implausible sensor readings should degrade confidence and be visible to operators.

title: Action auditability
excerpt: Operator decisions, automated recommendations, and evidence snapshots should be attributable and tamper-evident.

title: Operator feedback loop
excerpt: False alarms, useful alerts, and operator rationale should be retained to tune safety intelligence without hiding uncertainty.

title: Adjacent-zone risk
excerpt: Spatial adjacency should be considered when active work in one zone can affect workers or equipment nearby.
